diagrams.title: Unix File System Disk Layout
diagrams.description: Shows the physical organization of a Unix file system on disk
elements: Data Block Bitmap (allocation status of data blocks)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how file system structures are physically organized on disk and the order of components

diagrams.title: Inode Structure with Multi-level Indexing
diagrams.description: Illustrates how an inode stores pointers to data blocks using direct, single indirect, double indirect, and triple indirect pointers
elements: Direct Pointers (12 blocks - direct access to 48KB)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how file systems support files larger than the direct pointer capacity through hierarchical indexing

diagrams.title: File Allocation Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Three different approaches to storing file data blocks on disk
elements: Indexed: Index block contains pointers to all data blocks at any locations
diagrams.purpose: Comparing the three main file allocation strategies showing how they differ in block organization
